channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump names Billy Long as IRS head
item.description: Trump said his relationship with Long, who currently works as a business and tax adviser, dates back to 2011.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: Judge considers first lawsuit to overturn Missouri's near-total abortion ban
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Abortion-rights advocates on Wednesday asked a judge to overturn Missouri’s near-total ban on the procedure, less than a month after voters backed an abortion-rights constitutional amendment. Jackson County Circuit Judge Jerri Zhang did not immediately rule after the hearing.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two children wounded and gunman dead after shooting at Northern California school
item.description: PALERMO, Calif. (AP) — Two children were wounded Wednesday in a shooting at a tiny religious K-8 school in Northern California and deputies arrived to find the gunman on the ground near the playground, dead after apparently shooting himself, sheriff’s officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taylor Swift 'Eras' tie-in book is a smash, selling more than 800,000 copies in first week
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just one retail chain was enough for Taylor Swift to have the top-selling book last week and the biggest opening week of 2024. According to Circana, Swift's “Eras Tour Book” sold 814,000 copies over the Thanksgiving holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump picks Monica Crowley for senior State Department post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday nominated Monica Crowley, a former Fox News contributor who faced public scrutiny during Trump’s first term over allegations of plagiarism, for a top public relations post in the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth nomination sinking fast in the Senate
item.description: Senate Republicans say Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s choice to head the Defense Department, faces a very tough path to confirmation in the Senate and his bleak prospects have been communicated directly to the Trump transition team, which is now mulling other options.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Police hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO's masked killer after 'brazen, targeted' attack on NYC street
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A gunman killed UnitedHealthcare’s CEO on Wednesday in a “brazen, targeted attack” outside a Manhattan hotel where the health insurer was holding its investor conference, police said, setting off a massive search for the fleeing assailant hours before the annual Rockefeller Center Ch
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KAREN MATTHEWS and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Memphis police use excessive force and discriminate against Black people, Justice Department finds
item.description: The Memphis Police Department uses excessive force and discriminates against Black people, according to the findings of a U.S. Department of Justice investigation launched after the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Sainz And Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jury revisits key videos in NYC subway chokehold death trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jurors asked Wednesday to review police and bystander video at the heart of the chokehold manslaughter case against Daniel Penny as his lawyers complained that the Marine veteran was being harassed outside the New York City courthouse.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Key things to know about the fatal attack on the UnitedHealthcare CEO in NYC
item.description: The chief executive of UnitedHealthcare, one of the nation's largest insurers, was killed Wednesday in midtown Manhattan in what police described as a targeted attack by a shooter outside a hotel where the company was holding a conference.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Gail Slater to lead DOJ antitrust division
item.description: Gail Slater will take over the DOJ's high-profile antitrust cases against tech giants like Google and Apple.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Takeaways from Biden's Africa trip: Pardon of son Hunter overshadows official business
item.description: LUANDA, Angola (AP) — President Joe Biden's long-delayed trip to Africa had many of the hallmarks of a traditional state visit: There was a 12-shot cannon salute. A series of warm handshakes with Angolan President João Lourenço. Celebratory music. Photo opportunities.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Underground voids from abandoned mines pose danger to people and property when land collapses
item.description: Swaths of Pennsylvania and many other states are honeycombed with old, unstable mines that can cause the earth to suddenly give way.
item.dc:creator: Michael Rubinkam And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ohio's new law aimed at 'hooning' takes effect
item.description: Ohio's new law, increases penalties for stunt driving, street racing, and evading police, and allows for the seizure of vehicles involved, in response to growing concerns over hazardous driving practices and loud traffic disturbances across the state.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Thompson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court Latest: Majority of justices appear likely to uphold state ban on transgender care
item.description: The Supreme Court heard arguments on Wednesday in just its second major transgender rights case, a challenge to a Tennessee law that bans gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An ex-officer who died in an apparent suicide before his federal trial was barred from having a gun
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A former police detective in Kansas who died in an apparent suicide as he was about to stand trial on civil rights violations for allegedly sexually assaulting and terrorizing vulnerable women for decades wasn't supposed to have a gun while he was under house arrest.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trooper, the dog abandoned on I-75 before Hurricane Milton, finds forever home
item.description: Trooper, the dog who was rescued by the Florida Highway Patrol during Hurricane Milton has now been adopted, the Leon County Humane Society announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KRON4

item.title: Harris found success with women who have cats, but Trump got the dog owner vote: AP VoteCast
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The lead-up to the 2024 election was all about cat owners. But in the end, the dogs had their day.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, HUMERA LODHI and ANNIE NG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi's family says search is 'far from over' after police reveal she voluntarily left for Mexico
item.description: Authorities may consider Hannah Kobayashi a "voluntary missing person," but her family isn't done looking for her.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania train slams into milk truck, video shows
item.description: A train was filmed slamming into a truck that appeared to be stuck on the tracks at a railroad crossing in snowy Erie, Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alleged Delta stowaway disrupts return flight to New York, video shows
item.description: A woman who allegedly stowed away on a Delta Air Lines flight to Paris was filmed disrupting a return flight to New York after French officials denied her entry to the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump team outlines inauguration benefits for donors
item.description: The team highlighted that committee members who give $1 million or raise $2 million receive six tickets to access exclusive inaugural events between Saturday, Jan. 18, and Monday, Jan. 20, the day of the swearing-in.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Community 'college deserts' leave students stranded from higher education
item.description: College deserts have gained more attention in recent years as they are considered a significant reason some groups are not attending college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO fatally shot in 'targeted attack' in NYC, officials say
item.description: The CEO of UnitedHealthcare was killed in a Midtown shooting Wednesday morning, sources have confirmed to Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KTLA 5

item.title: Spotify Wrapped: How to view, share your personalized recap
item.description: Want to show everyone who your top five artists were or just how much time you spent listening to music and podcasts? Now is your chance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Max to begin cracking down on password sharing next week
item.description: Max will begin its password-sharing crackdown next week, beginning with "very early, gentle messaging" directed at high-usage users.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Police say searchers don’t expect to find woman in Pennsylvania sinkhole alive
item.description: The search for a woman who is believed to have fallen into a sinkhole in western Pennsylvania has become a recovery effort after two treacherous days of digging through mud and rock produced no signs of life.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No winner for Mega Millions, jackpot grows to $579 million
item.description: No one won the Mega Millions drawing on Tuesday night. The jackpot has now grown to an estimated $579 million ahead of the next drawing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, KRON4

item.title: Students at Ohio elementary school can participate in Satanic Temple's religious learning program
item.description: The Satanic Temple is offering religious learning instruction to students at an Ohio elementary school as part of the state's religious release program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Frontier Airlines to roll out first-class seating, other perks in 2025
item.description: Frontier Airlines will offer first-class seating for the first time in its history starting in late 2025, the company announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-ballerina convicted of killing husband in 'Black Swan' case gets 20 years in prison
item.description: Ashley Benefield, who was found guilty of manslaughter for shooting her husband during a domestic argument in 2020 sentencing hearing is set for today.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, ABC 4

item.title: E. coli outbreak linked to McDonald's onions is over: CDC
item.description: An outbreak across 14 states in October connected to McDonald’s slivered onions from the popular menu item impacted at least 100 people.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, KRON4

item.title: Former SmileDirectClub customers may be eligible for a refund
item.description: When SmileDirectClub shut down a year ago, scores of existing customers of the teeth-straightening company were left in limbo. Now, tens of thousands are set to get some relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Christian McCaffrey's season 'potentially' over after knee injury in 49ers' loss to Bills
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — San Francisco running back Christian McCaffrey suffered a knee injury that coach Kyle Shanahan described as “potentially” season-ending in the 49ers’ 35-10 loss to the Buffalo Bills on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, WGN 9

item.title: DNA test brings NY father, daughter together after nearly 50 years
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Thanksgiving is more than a tradition – it's a time for loved ones and families to get together, to embrace and give thanks for all that is good in their lives. For one family in Rockland County, this is a special holiday. A daughter and her biological father are together.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, WGN 9

item.title: A California woman was called into work on her day off. She left a lottery winner
item.description: Nobody likes being called into work on their day off. But for one California woman, a last-minute call-in would ultimately change her life for the better.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9
